response.justification: The candidate is a strong generalist software engineer but is a poor match for a mid-senior C++ capital-markets role. C++ exposure is limited to roughly six months at National Instruments (2014-2015) plus a university thesis project, far short of the required 5-10 years of hands-on C++ on Windows. There is no Fixed Income or Fixed Income Derivatives experience, no Summit platform, and only generic SQL listed without SQL Server or Oracle depth. Although RIDE Capital provides brief fintech/investment exposure, that work was Node/TypeScript, not C++ in a capital-markets engineering context. The core mandatory and domain requirements are not met.
strengths: Broad, long-running software engineering career with strong fundamentals and TDD/CI-CD practices
Some financial-domain exposure via RIDE Capital investment platform work
Fluent communicator with a track record of teaching, talks, and workshops
concerns: C++ experience is minimal (~6 months) versus the required 5-10 years on Windows
No Fixed Income / capital-markets C++ domain experience; relevant work was Node/TypeScript
No demonstrated SQL Server/Oracle depth or Summit/messaging-framework experience
